pattern: When picks must be distinct in value, collapsing to the unique values and greedily taking the largest yields the maximum sum.
hints: Duplicates can never both be chosen, so they add nothing beyond a single copy.
After keeping one copy of each value, which ones maximize the sum?
Deduplicate, sort the distinct values descending, and emit the first k of them.

edge_inputs.description: a duplicated value cannot be picked twice
k exceeds distinct count, so fewer are returned
k equals one picks only the maximum

stress.description: many values with duplicates, random k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randrange(1, 200) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randrange(1, n + 1)
    return [nums, k]
